content: The intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) that power autonomous curious agents can be re-purposed to prioritize the consolidation of active learning agent memory: new memory traces that carry high prediction error (the same signal that drives curiosity) get moved into long-term persistent storage, while low-error redundant memories are pruned automatically. This creates a self-organizing memory system that aligns what the agent retains with what it still needs to learn, eliminating the need for manual memory retention scheduling.
